Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Plaintiffs Action
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HANDY TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Stay Plaintiffs Action is granted. This case is stayed pending conclusion of the Zenelaj case or further order of the court. The stay is granted per the mandatory provision of CCP 1281.4 and per the discretionary principles set forth in Caiafa Professional Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. (1993) 15 Cal. App. 4th 800. Section 1281.4 requires the stay of this action since arbitration has been ordered in the Zenelaj case and the plaintiffs in that case allege the same PAGA claim representing the same or many of the same workers as is alleged by plaintiff Juanita Washington in this case. Moreover, even if a stay were not mandated by section 1281. 4, judicial economy, comity and avoidance of potentially inconsistent rulings strong favor issuance of a discretionary stay. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)
